2_1_core_thesis.statement: In a landscape where volcanic nature is uncertain, JVTO sells operational certainty—built on disciplined safety, documented proof, and local execution.
2_1_core_thesis.positioning: Private police-led operator — safety discipline + verification proof
2_1_core_thesis.unique_mechanism: Active Tourist Police founder × Ijen health screening × proof-first trust architecture
2_1_core_thesis.market_differentiation: Not price-competitive; operationally differentiated through safety discipline and legal/police transparency

2_3_narrative_claimmap._meta: 9 canonical claims — each claim anchored to mechanism, evidence, and primary page

c1_safety_led_operations.claim_text: Volcano travel is unpredictable, so JVTO focuses on operational certainty: disciplined decisions, clear safety boundaries, and realistic expectations.
mechanism: Risk-aware timing and route planning (night drives, steep hikes, long transfers).
Respect for official closures and safety instructions.
Plan B framework to keep the trip valuable when conditions change.
c1_safety_led_operations.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO focuses on operational certainty for East Java volcano routes: risk-aware planning, safety boundaries, and Plan B alternatives.
c1_safety_led_operations.nlp_short: Safety-led planning. Clear boundaries. Plan B when conditions change.
c1_safety_led_operations.nlp_cs_reply: We can't control weather or volcanic conditions, but we do control planning and safety decisions. If conditions change, we use Plan B options to keep your trip safe and worthwhile.

c2_private_tours_execution_control.claim_text: JVTO runs private tours by default so timing, pacing, and safety decisions stay consistent for your group.
mechanism: Dedicated vehicle and crew for your booking (not mixed with other travellers).
Fewer compromises on timing, stops, and recovery time.
Simpler coordination for early starts and long transfers.
c2_private_tours_execution_control.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO is private-tour focused for East Java volcano trips, supporting better time control and consistent execution.
c2_private_tours_execution_control.nlp_short: Private tours only. Your own vehicle. Your own pace (within safety limits). No mixed groups by default.
c2_private_tours_execution_control.nlp_cs_reply: We operate private tours by default, so your schedule isn't affected by other groups. It helps with timing, comfort, and safer decision-making.

c3_all_inclusive_clarity_reduce_surprise.claim_text: JVTO uses an all-inclusive structure by default to reduce surprise costs and mid-trip negotiation.
mechanism: Clear inclusions/exclusions in writing (voucher is the booking reference).
Less on-road negotiation for tickets and logistics.
Payment steps and cancellation rules are centralized in policy pages.
c3_all_inclusive_clarity_reduce_surprise.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO emphasizes written inclusions/exclusions and centralized policies to reduce uncertainty around pricing and changes.
c3_all_inclusive_clarity_reduce_surprise.nlp_short: All-inclusive by default. Clear inclusions. Fewer surprise costs. Transparent pricing, written rules.
c3_all_inclusive_clarity_reduce_surprise.nlp_cs_reply: Most costs are bundled so your trip runs smoothly. What's included and excluded is written clearly in your booking reference.

c4_ijen_health_screening_safety_layer.claim_text: On routes that include Mount Ijen, JVTO integrates health screening as a practical safety step, not a formality.
mechanism: Pre-hike checks aligned to access/safety controls (where required).
Clear guidance on what it is, why it matters, and what happens if a guest is not fit to climb.
Documentation flow is explained in the Travel Guide.
c4_ijen_health_screening_safety_layer.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO includes an Ijen health-screening protocol on itineraries that include Mount Ijen, documented in the Travel Guide.
c4_ijen_health_screening_safety_layer.nlp_short: Ijen routes include a health-screening layer (when required). Real screening, clear rules.
c4_ijen_health_screening_safety_layer.nlp_cs_reply: If your itinerary includes Ijen, we'll guide you through the health screening process (when required). It's a safety step before the hike — not a formality.

c5_proof_first_trust_verification_layer.claim_text: JVTO organizes trust signals as a verification layer: clear claims, context, and evidence links — not just marketing statements.
mechanism: Proof Library pages map claim → context → evidence.
Partners and memberships are explained by function (competence, sustainability context, verification).
Press and recognition are listed with context, not just logos.
c5_proof_first_trust_verification_layer.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO uses a proof-library approach: organizing verifiable credentials, partners, and recognition as structured trust signals.
c5_proof_first_trust_verification_layer.nlp_short: Proof you can verify — licenses, partners, press, and documented references. Trust stack, not slogans.
c5_proof_first_trust_verification_layer.nlp_cs_reply: If you want to verify us, start at /verify-jvto. It's organized so you can check credentials, partners, and references quickly.

c6_reviews_registry_independent_validati.claim_text: JVTO treats reviews as a registry: grouped by platform and theme, so guests can validate patterns instead of reading random quotes.
mechanism: Platform-based browsing (Google, Trustpilot, etc.).
Theme tags (safety, punctuality, guiding quality, logistics).
Link from reviews to crew profiles and common routes.
c6_reviews_registry_independent_validati.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO structures independent reviews by platform and theme to support auditability of guest experience claims.
c6_reviews_registry_independent_validati.nlp_short: Reviews as a registry, not a quote wall. Check patterns across platforms.
c6_reviews_registry_independent_validati.nlp_cs_reply: You can check reviews by platform and by theme at /why-jvto/reviews. It's organized so you can see patterns, not random quotes.

c7_our_team_personality_economy_operatio.claim_text: JVTO’s product is executed by real people. The Team Registry shows roles, routes, and proof from review mentions — not generic bios.
mechanism: Crew profiles focus on role, strengths, languages, and common routes.
Each profile links to reviews that mention the crew member.
Operational policies are kept on Standards/Policy pages to avoid duplication.
c7_our_team_personality_economy_operatio.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO publishes a team registry that connects staff roles and route expertise to review-based evidence.
c7_our_team_personality_economy_operatio.nlp_short: Meet the people behind your trip — with proof from reviews. Team profiles linked to real guest language.
c7_our_team_personality_economy_operatio.nlp_cs_reply: If you want to know who runs the trip, see /why-jvto/our-team. Each profile links to review mentions so you can judge from guest experience.

c8_partners_hpwki_indecon_isic_as_contex.claim_text: JVTO partnerships are explained by function: competence context (HPWKI), sustainability context (INDECON), and verification logic (ISIC).
mechanism: Each partner is described with: what it is, why it matters to guests, and how it is used (not just a logo).
Partner references are organized in the Proof Library.
Partner claims avoid overstatement; they provide context rather than guarantees.
c8_partners_hpwki_indecon_isic_as_contex.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO describes partnerships as functional trust signals (competence, sustainability context, verification) within the Proof Library.
c8_partners_hpwki_indecon_isic_as_contex.nlp_short: Partners for context: competence, sustainability, and verification. Not logos — real functions.
c8_partners_hpwki_indecon_isic_as_contex.nlp_cs_reply: Our partners are listed with context in /verify-jvto. It explains what each partnership means for your trip — not just a logo display.

c9_press_recognition_third_party_context.claim_text: JVTO lists press and recognition with context so guests can evaluate third‑party references, not just branding claims.
mechanism: Each mention is listed with what it supports (context), not as a blanket endorsement.
Where possible, include bibliographic or publication metadata to reduce ambiguity.
Press/recognition lives in Proof Library and links back to claims it supports.
c9_press_recognition_third_party_context.nlp_ai_snippet: JVTO maintains a press-and-recognition registry that links third-party references to specific claims and context.
c9_press_recognition_third_party_context.nlp_short: Press and recognition, explained with context. Third-party references you can evaluate.
c9_press_recognition_third_party_context.nlp_cs_reply: If you want third-party context on JVTO, see the Press & Recognition section in /verify-jvto/press-recognition.

_items: Private transport — AC MPV (1–3 guests) · Toyota Hiace minibus (4–9 guests) · Hiace + MPV (10–11 guests) — fuel, tolls, parking included
Dedicated crew — driver-guide (1–3 guests) · professional driver + escort guide (4–11 guests) · licensed local site guides at key locations
All entrance fees and permits to attractions
Mineral water — daily supply
Full pick-up to drop-off assistance
Complimentary JVTO travel T-shirt (1 per participant)
Meals as stated in itinerary (minimum: breakfast all packages)
Gas masks and trekking poles for Ijen Crater hike
Health screening coordination (where BBKSDA access rules require it)

Helmets at Madakaripura Waterfall are **not a JVTO inclusion** — they are provided by the local waterfall site management at the canyon entrance. JVTO guides accompany guests through the approach (wading, slippery stones, narrow canyon passages). The local site team handles safety equipment on-site. Do NOT list helmet as a JVTO-provided item for any Madakaripura route.
Private 4WD Jeep for Mount Bromo crater area — max ≈4 guests per jeep; additional jeeps for larger groups
Ferry crossing — Bali to Java (Gilimanuk-Ketapang)
International / domestic flights
Tips for drivers and guides (at guest discretion)
Personal expenses — snacks, souvenirs, laundry (recommend IDR 500,000/person minimum)
Meals not stated in itinerary
Travel insurance (optional but recommended)
Indonesian VISA (if applicable)

The aim is to reduce the risk of sending unfit guests into a demanding environment with sulphur gas and steep trails...

6_7_section_render_order._meta: 17-step sequence for all tour package pages
_steps: **Hero block** — Package name, duration, origin city, physicality rating
**Trust cue bar** — Police-led badge, private tour badge, Ijen screening badge (if ijen_relevant)
**Key highlights** — 3-5 bullet points: main destinations, timing, what makes it special
**Pricing table** — IDR/person by pax tier — all tiers shown
**Inclusions summary** — What is included — 8-item standard + route-specific additions
**Exclusions summary** — What is not included — 6-item standard list
**Itinerary** — Day-by-day: day title, activity summary, hotel, meals
**Hotels used** — Hotel names per night (multi-day packages only)
**Meals breakdown** — Breakfast/lunch/dinner count totals
**Health requirements** — Per-package fitness and health notes
**Ijen screening callout** — ONLY for ijen_relevant=True packages — links to /travel-guide/ijen-health-screening
**Closest alternative** — One link to closest alternative package
**Package FAQs** — 3-5 package-specific questions from faq_logic
**Booking CTA** — WhatsApp CTA +6282244788833 + booking flow summary
**Verify-before-book module** — Links to /verify-jvto — show trust credentials
**Policy summary** — Deposit 20%, cancellation 48h, bank accounts
**Reviews footer** — Link to /why-jvto/reviews + platform badges

booking_policy_answer_block.question: How do I book a JVTO tour?
booking_policy_answer_block.answer: Contact JVTO via WhatsApp (+6282244788833) with your dates, group size, origin (Surabaya or Bali), and preferred package. JVTO sends a tailored proposal. A 20% deposit secures the booking. Balance is due 7 days before Day 1. An E-Voucher is issued upon full payment. The E-Voucher governs all trip specifics.

cancellation_policy_answer_block.question: What is JVTO's cancellation policy?
cancellation_policy_answer_block.answer: Guests may cancel up to 48 hours before Day 1 of the tour. Cancellations within this window forfeit the deposit. For guest-initiated cancellations before the cutoff, JVTO converts the deposit to Travel Credit (valid for future bookings) rather than issuing a cash refund. Force majeure situations (closures, volcanic alerts, natural events) are handled separately.

ijen_health_screening_answer_block.question: What is the Ijen health screening at JVTO?
ijen_health_screening_answer_block.answer: For tours that include Mount Ijen, JVTO integrates a health screening step when crater access rules require it. Screening is conducted by a licensed medical professional (dr. Ahmad Irwandanu, SIP verified by Kemkes). It checks basic fitness for the overnight hike in a sulphur environment. If a guest is assessed as unfit, the Ijen portion of the itinerary may be adjusted or skipped. The screening is required when BBKSDA conditions meet the threshold defined in Surat Edaran SE.1658/KSA.9/2024 — it is not universally mandatory on every visit.

private_tours_answer_block.question: Are JVTO tours private or shared?
private_tours_answer_block.answer: All JVTO tours are private only. Each booking has a dedicated vehicle, driver, and guide — not shared with other guests. There are no "seat in coach" or group departure options. Private-only operation means timing, pacing, and safety decisions are made for your group alone.

closures_weather_answer_block.question: What happens if Bromo or Ijen is closed on my dates?
closures_weather_answer_block.answer: JVTO follows official instructions from Indonesian authorities (PVMBG, BBKSDA, park management). Depending on the situation, JVTO may: (1) adjust timings or use alternative viewpoints, (2) replace a closed stop with other available destinations, (3) issue Travel Credit if conditions make the core itinerary impossible. JVTO does not guarantee specific conditions — volcanic access is always subject to official status.

trust_legitimacy_answer_block.question: How can I verify that JVTO is a legitimate operator?
trust_legitimacy_answer_block.answer: JVTO operates under PT Java Volcano Rendezvous (NIB 1102230032918, registered AHU). The founder Agung Sambuko is an active Tourist Police officer (Ditpamobvit). JVTO maintains a public proof library at /verify-jvto covering: legal credentials, police documents (SPRIN), press coverage (Detik.com, Radar Jember 2021), historical artifacts (2015 Booking.com Award, Stefan Loose guidebook). Independent ratings: 4.8/5 Trustpilot (47 reviews).

safety_on_tours_answer_block.question: How does JVTO approach safety on volcano tours?
safety_on_tours_answer_block.answer: JVTO's safety framework has three layers: (1) Risk-aware planning — timing decisions account for night drives, steep hikes, long transfers, and cold temperatures. (2) Official closure compliance — JVTO follows PVMBG, BBKSDA, and park authority instructions without exception. Guests are not taken to restricted areas. (3) Plan B — every itinerary has a documented alternative if a primary destination is inaccessible. Guests are briefed on what the alternative is before the tour begins.

packing_fitness_answer_block.question: What should I pack for JVTO volcano tours?
packing_fitness_answer_block.answer: The core list for most JVTO routes: layered clothing (cold pre-dawn, warmer later), closed shoes with grip (hiking shoes for Ijen and Tumpak Sewu), headlamp with spare batteries, small daypack, water bottle, light rain layer, and any personal medication. For Ijen: a gas mask is supplied at the crater — you do not need to bring one. Fitness: Ijen requires basic cardiovascular fitness (3 km night hike, sulphur environment). Tumpak Sewu requires moderate lower-body strength for the canyon descent. Bromo is accessible to most fitness levels by 4WD jeep.

police_escort_answer_block.question: Can JVTO arrange a police escort for large groups?
police_escort_answer_block.answer: For large groups (typically around 18 guests or more), JVTO can help coordinate an official traffic police (Patwal) escort on certain road segments — for example, from a toll exit to Bondowoso — if approved by the relevant Traffic Police unit. This is coordinated through the founder's institutional police relationships. It is not available for standard small-group bookings and requires advance planning. Contact JVTO via WhatsApp to discuss eligibility.

isic_student_pricing_answer_block.question: Does JVTO offer student discounts?
isic_student_pricing_answer_block.answer: JVTO is an ISIC provider (ID 259268). Verified international students holding a valid ISIC card may access adapted rates on selected packages. The discount is applied to keep travel fair for students who face higher foreign visitor entrance fees at Indonesian national parks, without reducing safety or quality. Eligibility must be verified before booking. See /isic/student-package for current terms.

canonical_pages.ai_summary_snippet: Kawah Ijen (2,769m): volcanic crater lake in East Java famous for Blue Fire phenomenon and sulfur mining. Health screening may be required per BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024.
canonical_pages.answer_block: Kawah Ijen is an active volcanic crater lake at 2,769m altitude in East Java, Indonesia. Famous for the rare Blue Fire (sulfuric gas combustion) phenomenon visible 2-4am and active sulfur mining. Difficulty: moderate. Hazard: SO₂ up to 50,000 ppm — gas masks required (provided by JVTO). Health screening may be required per BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024. Blue Fire is subject to weather and gas conditions — not guaranteed.

canonical_pages.ai_summary_snippet: Mount Bromo (2,329m): iconic active volcano in East Java's Tengger Caldera. Sunrise viewing, 4WD jeep required for the sea of sand approach.
canonical_pages.answer_block: Mount Bromo is an active volcano at 2,329m in East Java's Tengger Caldera (part of Bromo Tengger Semeru National Park). Famous for dramatic sunrise over the sea of sand and the smoking crater rim. Difficulty: moderate (250 steps to crater rim). 4WD jeep required for the sand sea approach — included in all JVTO Bromo packages. May close during Yadnya Kasada ceremony.

canonical_pages.answer_block: Madakaripura Waterfall is a sacred canyon waterfall at 620m in East Java (Probolinggo area). Considered one of Indonesia's tallest waterfalls. Difficulty: moderate. Helmets are provided by local site management. Frequently included as a same-day add-on to Bromo in JVTO 2D1N, 3D2N, and longer packages.

homepage_entity_summary.tagline: Private. All-inclusive. Tourist Police-led. Bromo, Ijen, Tumpak Sewu from Surabaya and Bali.
homepage_entity_summary.description_60_words: Java Volcano Tour Operator (JVTO) is a registered Indonesian travel agency offering private all-inclusive tours to East Java's volcanic destinations. Led by an active Tourist Police officer, JVTO runs 16 packages — 12 from Surabaya, 4 from Bali — covering Bromo, Ijen Crater, Tumpak Sewu, Madakaripura, and Papuma Beach. All tours are private, never shared. Rated 4.8/5 on Trustpilot (47 reviews).
key_facts: Registered: PT Java Volcano Rendezvous, NIB 1102230032918, TDUP issued 2023-02-11
Founder: Agung Sambuko — active Tourist Police officer (Ditpamobvit East Java)
16 private packages: 12 from Surabaya (1D1N–6D5N), 4 from Bali (3D2N–5D4N)
Address: Jl. Khairil Anwar No.102 A, Badean, Bondowoso, East Java 68214

four_pillars.one_liner: Founder is an active Tourist Police officer (Ditpamobvit East Java). Routes follow official closures without exception. Backup plans always prepared.
Every booking gets a dedicated private vehicle and guide. No shared groups, ever. Better timing control and simpler coordination.
Transport, guide, entrance fees, hotel, meals, gas masks, 4WD jeep — all included. Written inclusions/exclusions policy. No on-road negotiations.
Every credential is publicly verifiable: legal permits, police documents, BBKSDA authorization, partner proofs, press coverage — all at /verify-jvto with SHA-256 hashes.

blocks.question: Do you need a health certificate for Ijen Crater?
blocks.answer: Ijen Crater access rules (per BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024) can require a local doctor's health certificate before entry. JVTO coordinates the clinic workflow the evening before the trek — guests only need to bring their passport. The screening checks blood pressure and oxygen saturation. When required, JVTO handles all coordination.

blocks.question: Is Ijen Blue Fire guaranteed?
blocks.answer: Blue Fire is a natural phenomenon caused by igniting sulfuric gases — it is subject to weather, gas activity, and visibility conditions. JVTO does not guarantee Blue Fire visibility. The phenomenon is typically visible 2-4am during clear nights with stable gas activity.

blocks.question: Can Ijen or Bromo be closed?
blocks.answer: Yes. Bromo may close for the Yadnya Kasada Hindu ceremony (dates vary annually) and for elevated volcanic activity. Ijen may close for elevated SO₂ levels or volcanic events. JVTO follows all official BKSDA and park authority closures without exception. Backup route plans are always prepared.

blocks.question: What safety equipment does JVTO provide?
blocks.answer: JVTO provides: gas masks and trekking poles for Ijen Crater (mandatory — SO₂ can reach 50,000 ppm); private 4WD jeep for Bromo sand sea approach; helmets at Madakaripura provided by local site management. All routes are planned to follow official safety boundaries.

blocks.question: How do I book a JVTO tour?
blocks.answer: To book: (1) WhatsApp +6282244788833 or email hello@javavolcano-touroperator.com to inquire and confirm your package; (2) Pay 20% deposit by bank transfer to confirm reservation (BRI 001301001779564 or BCA 1200944352 — PT Java Volcano Rendezvous); (3) Pay balance before departure. 100% deposit required if travel is within 14 days.

blocks.question: What should I bring for Ijen Crater?
blocks.answer: For Ijen: warm clothing (summit can reach 5-15°C), trekking shoes with grip, headlamp (required for 2am start), passport (required for health screening coordination), and personal medication. Gas masks and trekking poles are provided by JVTO — you do not need to bring your own.

blocks.question: What is JVTO's cancellation policy?
blocks.answer: Cancellation 48 hours or more before departure: 100% travel credit, non-expiring and transferable to any traveler. Cancellation under 48 hours: deposit is forfeited. No cash refunds — travel credit only. The credit can be applied to any future JVTO booking.

blocks.question: How much deposit does JVTO require?
blocks.answer: JVTO requires a 20% deposit at time of booking to confirm your reservation. If your travel date is within 14 days of the booking, 100% payment is required upfront. Payment is by bank transfer only: BRI 001301001779564 or BCA 1200944352 (PT Java Volcano Rendezvous).

blocks.question: What is included in JVTO's tour price?
blocks.answer: All JVTO tours include: private air-conditioned transport (MPV for 1-3 guests, Minibus for 4-11 guests), English-speaking guide, all entrance fees and permits, gas masks and trekking poles (Ijen routes), private 4WD jeep (Bromo), quality hotel accommodation with daily breakfast, meals as per itinerary, mineral water, travel T-shirt, and full assistance from arrival to final drop-off.

blocks.question: What is NOT included in JVTO's tour price?
blocks.answer: Exclusions: international and domestic flights, Indonesian VISA (if applicable), travel insurance (optional but recommended), meals not listed in the itinerary, personal expenses (recommended: carry at least IDR 500,000/person for snacks, souvenirs, beverages), and tips for drivers and guides.

blocks.question: Are JVTO tours private or shared?
blocks.answer: All JVTO tours are fully private. You will never be grouped with other travelers. Your booking gets a dedicated vehicle, dedicated driver, and dedicated guide throughout the entire itinerary. This applies to all 16 packages — both Surabaya and Bali departures.

blocks.question: Is health screening required for Ijen?
blocks.answer: Ijen access rules (per BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024) can require a local health certificate before entry. When required, JVTO coordinates the clinic workflow the evening before the trek — you only need to bring your passport. It is not always required, but JVTO is prepared to coordinate it when the rules apply.

destinations.name: Kawah Ijen (Ijen Crater)
destinations.ai_extract: Kawah Ijen is an active volcanic crater lake at 2,769m altitude in East Java, Indonesia. Famous for the rare Blue Fire (Ijen Blue Fire) phenomenon — ignited sulfuric gases visible 2-4am — and active sulfur mining. Difficulty: moderate. Gas masks required (provided by JVTO). Health screening may be required per BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024 access rules. Blue Fire is a natural phenomenon — not guaranteed.

destinations.name: Mount Bromo
destinations.ai_extract: Mount Bromo is an active volcano at 2,329m in East Java's Tengger Caldera (Bromo Tengger Semeru National Park). Famous for dramatic sunrise views over the sea of sand and the smoking crater rim. Difficulty: moderate (250 steps to crater rim). Private 4WD jeep required for the sand sea approach — included in all JVTO Bromo packages. May close for Yadnya Kasada ceremony.

destinations.name: Madakaripura Waterfall
destinations.ai_extract: Madakaripura Waterfall is a sacred canyon waterfall at 620m in East Java (Probolinggo). One of Indonesia's tallest waterfalls at approximately 200m. Difficulty: moderate. Helmets provided by local site management. Historically associated with the Majapahit Kingdom. Frequently paired with Bromo as a same-day addition in JVTO packages.

key_facts.historical_significance: associated with Gajah Mada and Majapahit Kingdom

destinations.name: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
destinations.ai_extract: Tumpak Sewu is a dramatic curtain-style tiered waterfall at 500m in East Java (Lumajang). Difficulty: hard — steep descent path, technical terrain, requires physical fitness. The most challenging destination in JVTO's route network. Typically combined with Ijen, Papuma, and Bromo in 4D3N and longer packages.

destinations.name: Papuma Beach
destinations.ai_extract: Papuma Beach is a secluded beach on the southern East Java coast (Jember area) at 5m altitude. Difficulty: easy. Provides a coastal contrast element in JVTO's longer overland itineraries (4D3N and 5D4N). Not a standalone package destination — always combined with Ijen, Tumpak Sewu, and Bromo.

key_facts.role_in_itinerary: coastal contrast segment between volcanic and waterfall destinations
